content:
You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 1c1c77b0efb667cea80b532440b44cea1dc427c3 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2020 14:02:59 +0100
Subject: [PATCH] Fix BZ 64563 - additional payload length validation

https://bz.apache.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=64563

diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
index 58a3acd1a1..4bc218159a 100644
--- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
+++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
@@ -138,6 +138,14 @@
       </fix>
     </changelog>
   </subsection>
+  <subsection name="WebSocket">
+    <changelog>
+      <fix>
+        <bug>64563</bug>: Add additional validation of payload length for
+        WebSocket messages. (markt)
+      </fix>
+    </changelog>
+  </subsection>
   <subsection name="Web Applications">
     <changelog>
       <update>
-- 
2.34.1



diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
index 9412ffeb61..929822d94c 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
@@ -71,6 +71,7 @@ wsFrame.noContinuation=A new message was started when a continuation frame was e
 wsFrame.notMasked=The client frame was not masked but all client frames must be masked
 wsFrame.oneByteCloseCode=The client sent a close frame with a single byte payload which is not valid
 wsFrame.partialHeaderComplete=WebSocket frame received. fin [{0}], rsv [{1}], OpCode [{2}], payload length [{3}]
+wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid=An invalid WebSocket frame was received - the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload was illegally set
 wsFrame.sessionClosed=The client data cannot be processed because the session has already been closed
 wsFrame.suspendRequested=Suspend of the message receiving has already been requested.
 wsFrame.textMessageTooBig=The decoded text message was too big for the output buffer and the endpoint does not support partial messages

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
index 97c9c94b26..ba5813bc27 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
@@ -261,6 +261,13 @@ public abstract class WsFrameBase {
         } else if (payloadLength == 127) {
             payloadLength = byteArrayToLong(inputBuffer.array(),
                     inputBuffer.arrayOffset() + inputBuffer.position(), 8);
+            // The most significant bit of those 8 bytes is required to be zero
+            // (see RFC 6455, section 5.2). If the most significant bit is set,
+            // the resulting payload length will be negative so test for that.
+            if (payloadLength < 0) {
+                throw new WsIOException(
+                        new CloseReason(CloseCodes.PROTOCOL_ERROR, sm.getString("wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid")));
+            }
             inputBuffer.position(inputBuffer.position() + 8);
         }
         if (Util.isControl(opCode)) {

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`changelog.xml`**:
   - A new subsection for "WebSocket" was added to document the fix related to bug 64563. This entry states that additional validation of payload length for WebSocket messages has been implemented.

2. **`LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - A new string entry was added: `wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid`, which describes the error message for an invalid WebSocket frame where the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload is illegally set.

3. **`WsFrameBase.java`**:
   - In the method handling payload length, a validation check was added to ensure that the most significant bit of the 64-bit payload length is not set. If it is set, a `WsIOException` is thrown with a close reason indicating a protocol error, using the newly added error message from `LocalStrings.properties`.

### Relationships Between Modifications

- The addition in `changelog.xml` provides a high-level overview of the changes made in the codebase, specifically addressing bug 64563 related to WebSocket payload validation.
- The new error message in `LocalStrings.properties` supports the changes made in `WsFrameBase.java` by providing a user-friendly description of the error that occurs when the payload length validation fails.
- The code modification in `WsFrameBase.java` implements the actual logic for validating the payload length, ensuring that the application adheres to the WebSocket protocol as specified in RFC 6455. This change directly relates to the documentation in the changelog and the new error message, creating a cohesive update that enhances the WebSocket handling in the application.

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2020 14:02:59 +0100
Subject: [PATCH] Fix BZ 64563 - additional payload length validation

https://bz.apache.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=64563

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
index 9412ffeb61..929822d94c 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
@@ -71,6 +71,7 @@ wsFrame.noContinuation=A new message was started when a continuation frame was e
 wsFrame.notMasked=The client frame was not masked but all client frames must be masked
 wsFrame.oneByteCloseCode=The client sent a close frame with a single byte payload which is not valid
 wsFrame.partialHeaderComplete=WebSocket frame received. fin [{0}], rsv [{1}], OpCode [{2}], payload length [{3}]
+wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid=An invalid WebSocket frame was received - the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload was illegally set
 wsFrame.sessionClosed=The client data cannot be processed because the session has already been closed
 wsFrame.suspendRequested=Suspend of the message receiving has already been requested.
 wsFrame.textMessageTooBig=The decoded text message was too big for the output buffer and the endpoint does not support partial messages

diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
index 58a3acd1a1..4bc218159a 100644
--- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
+++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml
@@ -138,6 +138,14 @@
       </fix>
     </changelog>
   </subsection>
+  <subsection name="WebSocket">
+    <changelog>
+      <fix>
+        <bug>64563</bug>: Add additional validation of payload length for
+        WebSocket messages. (markt)

2.34.1



diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
index 97c9c94b26..ba5813bc27 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
@@ -261,6 +261,13 @@ public abstract class WsFrameBase {
         } else if (payloadLength == 127) {
             payloadLength = byteArrayToLong(inputBuffer.array(),
                     inputBuffer.arrayOffset() + inputBuffer.position(), 8);
+            // The most significant bit of those 8 bytes is required to be zero
+            // (see RFC 6455, section 5.2). If the most significant bit is set,
+            // the resulting payload length will be negative so test for that.
+            if (payloadLength < 0) {
+                throw new WsIOException(
+                        new CloseReason(CloseCodes.PROTOCOL_ERROR, sm.getString("wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid")));
+            }
             inputBuffer.position(inputBuffer.position() + 8);
         }
         if (Util.isControl(opCode)) {

### Modifications in Each File

1. **LocalStrings.properties**
   - Added a new string entry: 
     - `wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid`: This entry provides a message indicating that an invalid WebSocket frame was received due to the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload being illegally set.

2. **changelog.xml**
   - Introduced a new subsection under "WebSocket" in the changelog:
     - Documented the fix for bug 64563, which states that additional validation of payload length for WebSocket messages has been added.

3. **WsFrameBase.java**
   - Enhanced the payload length validation logic:
     - Added a check to ensure that the most significant bit of an 8-byte payload length is not set. If it is set, a `WsIOException` is thrown with a message indicating the specific protocol error related to the invalid frame.

### Relationships Between Modifications

- The addition of the new string in `LocalStrings.properties` is directly related to the changes made in `WsFrameBase.java`, as it provides a user-friendly error message for the new validation check implemented in the Java class.
- The entry in `changelog.xml` serves to document the changes made in both `WsFrameBase.java` and `LocalStrings.properties`, providing context for the bug fix (BZ 64563) and summarizing the enhancements made to the WebSocket payload validation process. This ensures that users and developers are aware of the new validation and its implications.

index 97c9c94b26..ba5813bc27 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
@@ -261,6 +261,13 @@ public abstract class WsFrameBase {
         } else if (payloadLength == 127) {
             payloadLength = byteArrayToLong(inputBuffer.array(),
                     inputBuffer.arrayOffset() + inputBuffer.position(), 8);
+            // The most significant bit of those 8 bytes is required to be zero
+            // (see RFC 6455, section 5.2). If the most significant bit is set,
+            // the resulting payload length will be negative so test for that.
+            if (payloadLength < 0) {
+                throw new WsIOException(
+                        new CloseReason(CloseCodes.PROTOCOL_ERROR, sm.getString("wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid")));
+            }
             inputBuffer.position(inputBuffer.position() + 8);
         }
         if (Util.isControl(opCode)) {

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
index 9412ffeb61..929822d94c 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/LocalStrings.properties
@@ -71,6 +71,7 @@ wsFrame.noContinuation=A new message was started when a continuation frame was e
 wsFrame.notMasked=The client frame was not masked but all client frames must be masked
 wsFrame.oneByteCloseCode=The client sent a close frame with a single byte payload which is not valid
 wsFrame.partialHeaderComplete=WebSocket frame received. fin [{0}], rsv [{1}], OpCode [{2}], payload length [{3}]
+wsFrame.payloadMsbInvalid=An invalid WebSocket frame was received - the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload was illegally set
 wsFrame.sessionClosed=The client data cannot be processed because the session has already been closed
 wsFrame.suspendRequested=Suspend of the message receiving has already been requested.
 wsFrame.textMessageTooBig=The decoded text message was too big for the output buffer and the endpoint does not support partial messages

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`changelog.xml`**:
   - A new subsection titled "WebSocket" was added to document the fix related to bug 64563. This entry states that additional validation of payload length for WebSocket messages has been implemented.

2. **`WsFrameBase.java`**:
   - An additional validation check was introduced to ensure that the most significant bit of the 64-bit payload length is not set, as per RFC 6455. If this bit is set, a `WsIOException` is thrown with a specific close reason indicating a protocol error.

3. **`LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - A new string entry was added to provide a message for the scenario where an invalid WebSocket frame is received due to the most significant bit of the payload being illegally set.

### Relationships Between Modifications

- The changes in `WsFrameBase.java` directly address the bug reported in BZ 64563 by implementing the necessary validation for the payload length in WebSocket messages. 
- The addition in `changelog.xml` serves to document this fix, providing visibility into the changes made for future reference.
- The update in `LocalStrings.properties` complements the code change by providing a user-friendly error message that can be used when the validation fails, enhancing the overall error handling and user experience related to WebSocket frame processing.

This could result in improper handling of WebSocket messages and potential protocol errors.
